title: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture
description:
The root directory of the Hadoop MapReduce project is responsible for managing the overall architecture and coordinating the execution of MapReduce jobs. It provides a high-level overview of the system and ensures that all components work together seamlessly.

The Cluster.java file is a key component of this directory, as it provides a way to access information about the map/reduce cluster. It initializes the clientProtocolProvider and client based on the configuration and jobTrackAddr. It also provides methods to get the file system, job, cluster status, task trackers, staging area directory, job history file path, queue ACLs, root queues, child queues, job tracker status, and task tracker expiry interval.

The Job.java file is another important component, as it manages and executes MapReduce jobs. It provides methods for setting up and monitoring the job, as well as for controlling its execution.

The protocol directory is responsible for defining the communication protocol between the client and the Hadoop MapReduce framework. It provides a set of classes and interfaces that enable the client to submit jobs for execution and retrieve job status and results. The ClientProtocol.java file is the key component of this directory, as it defines the ClientProtocol interface, which includes methods for submitting jobs, retrieving job status, and obtaining the latest profile for a job. The ClientProtocol interface is implemented by the JobClient class, which is the main entry point for submitting jobs and interacting with the Hadoop MapReduce framework.

The tools directory is responsible for handling command-line arguments and printing out usage information for each command. The files in this directory collaborate by receiving input from the user and processing it to perform the desired actions. The architectural role of this directory is to provide a user-friendly interface for interacting with the Hadoop MapReduce framework.
